channel.title: Cookie consent to block Google to track users
channel.description:
Hello everyone!
I know this (or similar) topic has been going through already in this community (https://community.frontity.org/t/disable-analytics-packages-until-users-accept-gdpr-cookie-consent/4304) but it has been quite a while since the topic was raised and I thought to ask again to see if anyone has meantime found a solution and would like to share it.
I have React Cookie Consent installed and since i live in the EU country where the "reject" button is required, I would need to block the Frontity Google Analytics package (or prevent Google to track users) when the visitor is clicking on the reject button. How would it be possible to implement such a thing?
The following
``` analytics: { pageviews: { googleAnalytics: false }, events: { googleAnalytics: false } } ``` Setting both pageviews and events false does not prevent for Google to track users. Any other ideas that can work maybe?
<!--
Are you asking about "how to solve a specific issue/error/problem in your Frontity project"?
If so, please check this guide on the information we suggest you include in your topic so the community (and the Frontity team) can provide the best possible help as quickly and as efficiently as possible
https://community.frontity.org/t/about-the-dev-talk-questions-category/2/3
The info you may provide: - Description of your issue - Specific errors you’re getting in the terminal or in the browser’s console - A repository with the code of your project - A deployed version of your site - The URL of your WP REST API
-->

item.title: Cookie consent to block Google to track users
item.dc:creator: Johan
item.description:
The moment you have set the Tracking ID (or Container ID when using GTM) it will add the scripts to the head (and body).

So no matter what you do, it will always trigger the initial tracking.

I’d guess the only solution would be a change in the analytics packages to check for an “am I allowed to track” state before injecting the script tag(s).
Big question will be; what’s the default? And; how to make stupid-proof without too much programming knowledge.

Read full topic
item.source: Cookie consent to block Google to track users

item.title: Cookie consent to block Google to track users
item.dc:creator: reinaldudras
item.description:
Hello everyone!

I know this (or similar) topic has been going through already in this community (Disable analytics packages until users accept GDPR cookie consent) but it has been quite a while since the topic was raised and I thought to ask again to see if anyone has meantime found a solution and would like to share it.

I have React Cookie Consent installed and since i live in the EU country where the “reject” button is required, I would need to block the Frontity Google Analytics package (or prevent Google to track users) when the visitor is clicking on the reject button. How would it be possible to implement such a thing?

The following

    analytics: {
      pageviews: {
        googleAnalytics: false
      },
      events: {
        googleAnalytics: false
      }
    }

Setting both pageviews and events false does not prevent for Google to track users. Any other ideas that can work maybe?

Read full topic
item.source: Cookie consent to block Google to track users
